Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation

Class FlixelAnimationStateMachine

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationStateMachine
public class FlixelAnimationStateMachine extends Object
Optional indirection between game logic state labels and registered animation clip names on a FlixelAnimationController.

What this is for

FlixelSprite stores clips on its FlixelAnimationController (from FlixelSprite.ensureAnimation()) when you call sprite.ensureAnimation().addAnimation or .addAnimationByPrefix. Those keys often match asset or sheet naming ("player_run", "hit_01") while your gameplay code thinks in terms of behavior ("running", "hurt").

This class keeps a map logicalState -> clipName. When you setState, it updates the remembered logical state, optionally notifies listeners, and if a mapping exists, calls FlixelAnimationController.playAnimation(String, boolean, boolean) so the correct clip plays. That lets you rename or reorganize clips in one place (the map) instead of hunting every playAnimation call.

What this is not

  • It does not register frames or build Animation; clips must already exist on the controller (via the owning FlixelSprite).
  • It does not poll input or physics; your code must call setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean) when the character's situation changes.
  • It does not auto-transition when a non-looping clip ends; listen to FlixelAnimationController.onAnimationFinished if you need that.
  • A setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean) to a state with no mapping still updates getState() and fires onStateChanged, but does not call playAnimation (useful for invisible states).

Typical usage

Hold one instance per character (or shared rules object). After loading graphics and registering clips on the sprite, wire the map once, then drive states from movement, AI, or timers. 


 // After player.loadGraphic(...) and player.ensureAnimation().addAnimation("walk", ...), etc.:
 FlixelAnimationStateMachine sm = new FlixelAnimationStateMachine();
 sm.mapStateToAnimation("idle", "idle");
 sm.mapStateToAnimation("run", "walk");
 sm.onStateChanged.add(s -> reactToLogicalState(s));  // optional: audio, VFX, etc.
 sm.setState("idle", player.ensureAnimation(), true, true);

 // Each frame or on events:
 if (onGround && speed > threshold) {
   sm.setState("run", player.ensureAnimation(), true, false);
 } else if (onGround) {
   sm.setState("idle", player.ensureAnimation(), true, false);
 }

Use forceRestart == false when you may call setState repeatedly for the same state (e.g. every frame while running) so the clip is not restarted from frame 0 each time. Use forceRestart == true when re-entering the same state should replay from the start (e.g. hurt stun).

FlixelSprite.update(float) must still run each frame so the FlixelAnimationController advances time and updates the drawn frame.

See Also:
  • FlixelAnimationController
  • FlixelSprite.ensureAnimation()

    Field
    Description
    final @NotNull FlixelSignal<String>
    onStateChanged
    Fired immediately after the internal state string changes in setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean), with the new state as payload.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clear()
    Resets logical state to empty and clears all mapStateToAnimation entries.
    @NotNull String
    getState()
    The last logical state set by setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean), or "" if none yet.
    void
    mapStateToAnimation(@NotNull String state, @NotNull String animationName)
    Associates a logical state label with an animation clip name that must already be registered on the sprite/controller (same string you passed to addAnimation / addAnimationByPrefix).

    Removes a mapping so setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean) for that label no longer triggers playAnimation.
    void

    Switches the logical state and, when newState differs from getState(), dispatches onStateChanged.

  • Field Details

    • onStateChanged

      @NotNull public final @NotNull FlixelSignal<String> onStateChanged
      Fired immediately after the internal state string changes in setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean), with the new state as payload. Not fired when setState is called with the same state as getState() (no-op).

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelAnimationStateMachine

      public FlixelAnimationStateMachine()

  • Method Details

    • mapStateToAnimation

      public void mapStateToAnimation(@NotNull @NotNull String state, @NotNull @NotNull String animationName)
      Associates a logical state label with an animation clip name that must already be registered on the sprite/controller (same string you passed to addAnimation / addAnimationByPrefix). Later setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean) calls for state will FlixelAnimationController.playAnimation(String, boolean, boolean) the given animationName.
      Parameters:
      state - arbitrary non-null key (e.g. "air", "crouch")
      animationName - clip key on the controller; if wrong, setState will not find a clip to play

    • removeState

      public void removeState(@NotNull @NotNull String state)
      Removes a mapping so setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean) for that label no longer triggers playAnimation. Does not change getState() by itself.

    • setState

      public void setState(@NotNull @NotNull String newState, @NotNull @NotNull FlixelAnimationController controller, boolean loop, boolean forceRestart)
      Switches the logical state and, when newState differs from getState(), dispatches onStateChanged. If stateToAnimation contains newState, calls FlixelAnimationController.playAnimation(String, boolean, boolean) on controller with the given loop and forceRestart.

      If newState equals the current state, returns immediately (no signal, no playAnimation).

      Parameters:
      newState - The logical state to enter.
      controller - The animation controller to use. Typically sprite.ensureAnimation(). Must be the controller that owns the mapped clips.
      loop - Forwarded to playAnimation (often true for idle/run, false for one-shot clips).
      forceRestart - Forwarded to playAnimation; use false when polling the same state every frame.

    • getState

      @NotNull public @NotNull String getState()
      The last logical state set by setState(java.lang.String, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.animation.FlixelAnimationController, boolean, boolean), or "" if none yet.

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Resets logical state to empty and clears all mapStateToAnimation entries. Does not stop or clear clips on any FlixelAnimationController; call FlixelAnimationController.clear() on the sprite if you need that.
